_doc: Per-fixture baselines for the v5 fixtures suite. Listed fixtures are expected to produce the EXACT `expected_errors` set (same count, same {file, line, column, code} per entry, optional message_contains substring). These fixtures test svelte2tsx's verbatim-emit behavior — they preserve user code character-for-character even when that user code doesn't type-check, so the errors below match what tsgo fires on the preserved source. Fixtures not in this list must produce zero errors to pass. Regenerate with `CAPTURE_BASELINES=1` on the runner when an emit change deliberately shifts the error shape; always `git diff` the result before committing.

runes-best-effort-types.v5.reason: let { g = foo } = $props() — `foo` is undefined; the fixture preserves the user's reference verbatim. svelte2tsx emits the same error in expectedv2.ts.

ts-runes-best-effort-types.v5.reason: Same as runes-best-effort-types but with extra TS-typed defaults (h = null as Bar, i = null as any as Baz). All three undefined references preserved verbatim.

ts-runes-generics.v5.reason: let x = $state<T>(0) where T is a generic. Real svelte's $state<T>(initial: T) signature flags this — 0 isn't assignable to generic T. svelte2tsx emits verbatim; our shim mirrors the real signature so the same TS2345 fires.

ts-runes-with-slot.v5.reason: Same $state<T>(0) pattern as ts-runes-generics, plus snippet handling.

snippet-module-hoist-2.v5.reason: import { imported } from './x' — './x' doesn't exist in the fixture. Tests that snippet hoisting works in the presence of unresolved imports; the import error itself is expected.

snippet-module-hoist-7.v5.reason: import { store } from './foo' — './foo' doesn't exist. Same pattern as snippet-module-hoist-2. Count raised from 1 to 2 after we started reporting the bad import on both the module-script declaration and the instance-script reference.

ts-runes-hoistable-props-5.v5.reason: import X from './X' — './X' doesn't exist. Tests prop type-extraction in presence of unresolved import.

ts-runes-hoistable-props-false-11.v5.reason: interface A { Abc: typeof a }; let {}: Abc = $props(). The TS2713 'A.Abc' inside the fixture's outer interface uses A as a namespace — svelte2tsx preserves and the user error is the test target.

await.v5.reason: `{await promise}` / `{#each await promise as item}` — await in mustache expressions. Our template emit doesn't wrap these in an async arrow so tsgo fires TS2304 on a referenced helper name. Tracked for v0.2; verbatim-preserved in upstream.

ts-await-generics.v5.reason: Now clean. Pre-2026-04-26 we emitted a spurious TS2304 (line 0:59) on a synthesized helper name referenced by the no-op {@render x(await promise)} branch; landing the proper render_tag emit (Round 2 #3 of notes/ARCH_PARITY.md) replaced that with a real `(x(await promise));` expression that type-checks correctly. Entry kept to document the history; expected_errors emptied so the fixture is now treated as clean.

snippet-generics.v5.reason: `{#snippet generic<T extends string>(val: T)}` — snippets with explicit generic parameters. Our snippet emit doesn't carry the generic list through to the synthesized arrow; `T` is unbound in the body. Real emit gap, deferred.

snippet-instance-script.v5.reason: Instance script body references `bar;` where `bar` is a snippet declared in the template. Upstream hoists snippet names into the body's declaration scope; we emit template-only. Deferred.

snippet-module-hoist-1.v5.reason: `import { imported } from './x'` — './x' doesn't exist. Same verbatim-preservation pattern as snippet-module-hoist-2; the higher count is from the second `./x` reference in this variant.

snippet-module-hoist-6.v5.reason: `<script module>export const foo = _foo</script>` with `_foo` declared as a snippet in the template. Upstream visibility-merges snippets into module scope via its hoist pipeline; we don't. Deferred.

ts-runes-hoistable-props-false-2.v5.reason: `interface Props { [index: typeof a]: boolean }` — index-signature key whose type is `typeof a` (a const). TS1268 per spec: index signature parameters must be `string | number | symbol | template literal`. User-code error preserved verbatim.

ts-runes-hoistable-props-false-5.v5.reason: `type Shadowed` declared in both `<script module>` and `<script lang="ts">` — duplicate identifier in the composed overlay. Our hoist emits both into the same module scope; upstream scopes module-script types separately. Deferred.

ts-runes-hoistable-props-false-7.v5.reason: `let { destructured } = {}; type Props = { prop: typeof destructured };` — property access on `{}` fires TS2339. User-code pattern, verbatim.

ts-runes-hoistable-props-false-14.v5.reason: `<script module>enum A {}</script>` + `<script lang="ts">interface A { Abc: number }</script>` — enum + interface share the same name across module and instance scripts. TS2567 fires because both get hoisted into the same overlay scope. Same root cause as hoistable-props-false-5; deferred.

ts-runes-hoistable-props-false-15.v5.reason: `import { store } from './foo'` with './foo' missing — verbatim preservation of unresolved import.
